text.title: pnpm-package-management Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for reproducible pnpm setup.

rule.statement: Pin the pnpm version in root package.json `packageManager` field and activate via `corepack enable`.
rule.rationale: Corepack runs the exact version on every dev machine + CI, eliminating "works on my laptop" lockfile drift.

rule.statement: Keep `shamefully-hoist=false` (default). Fix phantom-dep issues via `.pnpmfile.cjs` `readPackage` hook, never by hoisting.
rule.rationale: Hoisting hides bugs and creates downstream version conflicts; readPackage adds the missing dep declaratively.

rule.statement: Reference internal packages with the `workspace:*` protocol; use Changesets to rewrite to real versions at publish time.
rule.rationale: workspace:* breaks at publish unless Changesets rewrites it; the combo is the only safe pattern.

rule.statement: Use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI. Cache the pnpm store keyed by `pnpm-lock.yaml` hash.
rule.rationale: --frozen-lockfile blocks drift; store caching saves 30-60s per CI run.
Without the lockfile, every install is a fresh resolution; reproducibility lost.

rule.statement: packageManager pinned, lockfile committed, shamefully-hoist off, CI uses frozen-lockfile, workspace:* used.
pnpm not acceptable for the project (npm/yarn mandated, or Deno).
